meta.certificationName: Microsoft Certified: Fabric Data Engineer Associate (DP-700)
meta.title: MS-FDE-4: OneLake Design, Fabric Pipelines, and Real-Time Operations

questions.stem: A Fabric team wants one logical data lake shared by lakehouses, warehouses, and real-time items without copying files between services. Which Fabric component provides that foundation?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `OneLake` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[OneLake, the unified data lake - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-overview)

questions.stem: An engineer needs to expose external ADLS Gen2 data in Fabric without physically duplicating the files. What should the engineer create?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `A shortcut` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[OneLake shortcuts](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/onelake/onelake-shortcuts)

questions.stem: Why do Fabric lakehouse teams commonly store curated tables in Delta format rather than unmanaged CSV folders?

options.text: Delta tables provide managed table semantics and reliable updates for analytics workflows
Delta is required for every warehouse query
CSV cannot be stored in OneLake

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Delta tables provide managed table semantics and reliable updates for analytics workflows` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Delta Lake overview - Microsoft Fabric](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/delta-lake-overview)
